entries.definition:
(17:91) فَتُفَجِّرَ الْأَنْهَارَ خِلَالَهَا تَفْجِيرًا and you cause rivers to gush forth
through them forcefully.

فَجْر fajr [n.] 1 dawn (24:58) مِنْ قَبْلِ صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ before the dawn
prayer; * الفجر name of Sura 89, Meccan sura, so-named because
of the reference in verse 1 to the ‘Dawn’ 2 dawn light (2:187) حَتَّى
يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمُ الْخَيْطُ الْأَبْيَضُ مِنَ الْخَيْطِ الْأَسْوَدِ مِنَ الْفَجْرِ until the white streak of
dawn light becomes distinct for you from the black [lit. the white
thread of dawn becomes distinct from the black].
entries.definition_english:
(17:91) فَتُفَجِّرَ الْأَنْهَارَ خِلَالَهَا تَفْجِيرًا and you cause rivers to gush forth
through them forcefully.

فَجْر fajr [n.] 1 dawn (24:58) مِنْ قَبْلِ صَلَاةِ الْفَجْرِ before the dawn
prayer; * الفجر name of Sura 89, Meccan sura, so-named because
of the reference in verse 1 to the ‘Dawn’ 2 dawn light (2:187) حَتَّى
يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمُ الْخَيْطُ الْأَبْيَضُ مِنَ الْخَيْطِ الْأَسْوَدِ مِنَ الْفَجْرِ until the white streak of
dawn light becomes distinct for you from the black [lit. the white
thread of dawn becomes distinct from the black].

entries.definition:
f–j–w clear hole in a wall, (in a cave) open space, gap; to
make a hole. Of this root, فَجْوَة fajwatun, occurs once in the
Qur’an.

فَجْوَة fajwatun [n.] hole in a wall, open space (18:17) وَهُمْ فِي فَجْوَةٍ
مِنْهُ while they are in an open space of it [the cave].
entries.definition_english:
f–j–w clear hole in a wall, (in a cave) open space, gap; to
make a hole. Of this root, فَجْوَة fajwatun, occurs once in the
Qur’an.

فَجْوَة fajwatun [n.] hole in a wall, open space (18:17) وَهُمْ فِي فَجْوَةٍ
مِنْهُ while they are in an open space of it [the cave].
